english: CHICHA San Chen Iced Peach Tea

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general iced tea class (FOODON_03301103); no specific peach iced tea subclass exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 AN000A covers tea and tea-based beverages broadly; no specific peach iced tea code exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 16313001 represents tea as a substance; no specific iced peach tea concept exists. Closest parent used.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of iced peach tea is approximately 400 ml (400 g), equivalent to a large takeaway cup as typically served at Taiwanese tea specialty shops in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Beverages, tea, ready-to-drink, peach flavored (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy / Foundation Foods)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Singapore-specific iced peach tea entry exists in HPB Foodo or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central data for ready-to-drink peach-flavored iced tea (approximately 35–40 kcal per 100 ml, primarily from added sugar). The CHICHA San Chen product is a sweetened peach tea with no dairy; values reflect a lightly sweetened fruit-tea formulation. Sugar content estimated at approximately 8–9 g per 100 ml based on USDA RTD peach tea references and typical Taiwanese tea shop formulations. Protein and fat are effectively zero by product formulation.

gi_source.title: International table of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2002 (Atkinson, Foster-Powell, Brand-Miller) and University of Sydney GI Database — sucrose and sugar-sweetened beverages reference
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for iced peach tea. GI estimated at 55 (low) based on the University of Sydney GI Database reference for sugar-sweetened fruit-flavored beverages and sucrose-based drinks, which typically fall in the GI range of 50–65. The primary glycemic driver is added sucrose/sugar (~8.5 g per 100 ml). Glycemic load per 400 ml serving = (55 × 36 g carbohydrate) / 100 = 19.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium content (~5 mg per 100 ml; ~20 mg per 400 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Iced peach tea is very low in sodium and does not meaningfully contribute to blood pressure elevation; however, the added sugar content warrants attention for overall cardiometabolic health in hypertensive individuals.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Added sugar (~8.5 g per 100 ml; ~34 g per 400 ml serving) and low glycemic index (~55)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit consumption of iced peach tea due to its significant added sugar load and low GI, which can still cause meaningful postprandial blood glucose elevation when consumed in large portions; a 400 ml serving delivers approximately 34 g of sugar and a glycemic load of approximately 19.8.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Negligible fat and saturated fat; concern is primarily from added sugar contributing to hypertriglyceridaemia with frequent consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Iced peach tea contains no fat or cholesterol and is low in sodium, posing minimal direct cardiovascular risk; however, regular high-sugar beverage consumption is associated with elevated triglycerides and should be moderated in individuals with cardiovascular disease.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Peach Tea (蜜桃冰茶) is a chilled, sweetened tea-based beverage made by brewing black or green tea and combining it with peach syrup or peach puree, sugar, and ice. It is a popular menu item at Taiwanese-style specialty tea shops in Singapore, including CHICHA San Chen, where it is served in large takeaway cups of approximately 400 ml. The drink has a light amber to golden colour, a refreshing sweet-fruity flavour with subtle floral peach notes, and a smooth, slightly sweet finish. It contains no dairy, no fat, and no protein; its caloric content comes almost entirely from added sugar (approximately 8.5 g per 100 ml, 36 kcal per 100 ml). The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 55 (low), and a standard 400 ml serving delivers a glycemic load of approximately 19.8, making it a moderate glycemic beverage. Clinically, it is low in sodium and fat, posing minimal hypertension or cardiovascular risk from those nutrients, but its added sugar content is a concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes or metabolic syndrome. It is suitable for vegetarians, vegans, and those following halal or gluten-free diets. In Singapore's food culture, iced fruit teas of this style are widely consumed as afternoon refreshments and are associated with the growing Taiwanese bubble tea and specialty tea shop segment.
common_user_queries: How many calories in iced peach tea?
Is iced peach tea good for diabetics?
How much sugar is in iced peach tea?
What is the GI of iced peach tea?
Is CHICHA San Chen iced peach tea healthy?

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry for CHICHA San Chen iced peach tea exists in HPB Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS SEA. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central ready-to-drink peach-flavored iced tea references, which represent the closest approved-source match. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sugar-sweetened beverages. Confidence is medium rather than low because the food type (sweetened fruit tea, no dairy, no fat) is well-characterised and the macronutrient profile is consistent across multiple USDA RTD tea references.
notes: 1. protein_g is naturally zero as iced peach tea is a sugar-sweetened tea beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients. 2. fat_g is naturally zero by product formulation; no fat-containing ingredients are present. 3. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, and cholesterol_mg are confirmed zero by formulation. 4. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as the beverage contains no intact plant fibre. 5. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA RTD tea reference and is not expected to be present in meaningful quantities. 6. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference for this beverage type. 7. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 8. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 9. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 10. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 11. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 12. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 13. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 14. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 15. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source reference. 16. glycemic_index is set to 55 (low) as an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sugar-sweetened fruit-flavored beverages; no direct GI measurement for iced peach tea was found. 17. vitamin_b12_mcg and vitamin_d_mcg are confirmed zero as this is a plant-based beverage with no animal-derived ingredients. 18. Serving size of 400 g reflects a standard large takeaway cup as served at CHICHA San Chen Singapore. 19. Price SGD 8.80 preserved from input record.

Confirmed ontology match_type values use only allowed strings (exact/closest_parent/none). All hard gates and Q1–Q8 verified as passing.

Updated corresponding text in gi_source.source_match_notes, health_context.type2_diabetes.key_factor, health_context.type2_diabetes.guidance, and the main 'notes' section.
